Title: Ab Rauf says PH only mirrored federal unity in Melaka
Article:
AYER KEROH: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh has described Pakatan Harapan's inclusion in the state government as mere "decoration" to mirror the federal administration in Putrajaya, rather than an official partnership.
He said Barisan Nasional never promised to form a coalition state government with any party after its landslide victory in the 2021 Melaka state election.
However, following the cooperation formed at the federal level after the 15th General Election (GE15) in 2022, the state leadership adopted an inclusive approach by accommodating PH representatives in the administration.
"In reality, we did not form a government with any other party to support the administration after the state election. However, I adopted the understanding established in Putrajaya so it could be implemented in Melaka in 2022.
"What I want to emphasise is that their position was just a complement to the picture of unity portrayed in Putrajaya.
"We have managed the government administration in complete harmony and without prejudice until today," he said at a press conference after attending the Melaka State Legislative Assembly sitting at Seri Negeri here today.
Ab Rauf, who is also Melaka BN chairman, was commenting on PH's decision to
withdraw its support
for the state government today.
He said BN respected PH's right to make decisions based on their party principles, adding that differences in policy views would not be turned into personal enmity or used as an excuse to attack any quarter.
He said if there were any official decisions regarding positions in the state administration, they would be managed orderly, professionally, and in accordance with the laws and regulations in force.
At the same time, Ab Rauf assured that all state administrative matters, development projects, and public services would continue as usual.
"My priority is to ensure that any decision made does not weaken the people's mandate, open up room for abuse of power, or affect the stability of Melaka," he said.
He added that the ultimate goal of the administration must remain clear, which is to strengthen the state assembly, improve the quality of policies and debates, and deliver better benefits to the people.
"I cannot stop their party's decision because their presence in this government is like 'a marriage without a kadi, a divorce without a talak'... they are welcome to leave.
"This means we do not have an official agreement that allows us to claim 'matrimonial assets' because 'we sat on the wedding dais without a bridal platform'," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Families claim bodies as Bangkok bar fire death toll rises to 30
Article:
BANGKOK: Grieving relatives of victims of a deadly Bangkok bar fire collected their loved ones' bodies today as the death toll rose to 30, while others continued searching for missing family members.
Flames tore through the Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao bar and restaurant in the Thai capital late on Sunday during a live music performance, forcing patrons to flee, with some suffering severe burns.
Twenty-seven people were confirmed dead in the early hours of Monday, with another victim succumbing to injuries later that day.
By Tuesday morning, Bangkok's Chatuchak district office confirmed the death toll had risen to 30, with 75 people injured, including 24 in critical condition.
Relatives of the victims gathered at Police General Hospital on Tuesday to identify and collect the bodies of their loved ones.
At the rear of the hospital, grey coffins with gilded bas-reliefs were carried out as dozens of journalists looked on. Some coffins were placed in ambulances for transport to the victims' home provinces.
Nuttakarn "Mint" Sevoy, 30, arrived at the hospital to collect the body of her childhood friend Top Sarobol, who, like many others killed in the fire, was believed to have died from smoke inhalation.
Nuttakarn told AFP the family was "fortunate" that authorities were able to accurately identify him, as his body was not burned beyond recognition.
However, she said his family, especially his grandmother, was struggling to come to terms with the loss.
"She kept saying that it should have been him to cremate her... he should be the one attending her funeral, not her coming to collect her grandson's body like this," Nuttakarn said.
Jarunee Phosawad travelled from Roi Et in eastern Thailand to oversee the cremation of her cousin Kochaporn Sotharat and the return of her ashes.
"She was a joyful person," the 46-year-old told AFP, adding: "I felt cold when I heard she was dead."
Jarunee said Kochaporn, a single mother, had a daughter who had also travelled to Bangkok to undergo a DNA test.
"I do not know how she will live after this; she only had her mother."
For some families, however, hope remains.
Booyaporn Sermsiri was at the hospital searching for her 25-year-old daughter, Jarawee "Cartoon" Sermsiri, who remains missing.
"Since we haven't found her yet, we can only wait. We are holding onto hope," she told AFP.
She said Cartoon's friend contacted her early Monday morning, saying there had been a fire and she could not reach her.
Booyaporn, 51, said she had provided a DNA sample and was told by police that the results could be available later Tuesday.
"I am praying. Praying that she is alright," she said.
Forensic police have been examining the wreckage as authorities investigate the cause of the fire and why it became so deadly, including the possibility of an electrical fault and whether emergency exits were blocked.
Several witnesses said they saw smoke inside the premises before midnight, followed by a power outage, an explosion and intense flames that sent patrons fleeing.
Relatives of victims and survivors also visited the charred bar on Tuesday, with some laying garlands and praying for those who died.
Usa Tadsri, 40, survived the fire, but two friends who were with her were killed.
Namtip Tubsook and her husband Suwitcha Sirililung, who died in the blaze, left behind two children aged four and five, who are now being cared for by their grandmother, Nid.
"We were only looking to enjoy ourselves, but we ended up trapped in a disaster," Usa told reporters.
—AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asean envoy meets Myanmar opposition groups in Thailand
Article:
BANGKOK: Asean's special envoy on Myanmar held talks with ethnic minority rebel groups and a government-backed negotiation committee in an effort to tackle a civil war, with both sides expressing openness to dialogue, the Philippines said on Tuesday.
The talks took place in Thailand on Monday and followed a separate meeting a day earlier between foreign ministers of the 11-member Asean and their Myanmar counterpart, their first face-to-face talks since a 2021 military coup in Myanmar that triggered nationwide conflict.
Myanmar's army-backed leadership has been barred from top-level Asean meetings over their failure to comply with Asean's five-year-old "Five-Point Consensus" peace initiative, but some members of the bloc hope Sunday's meeting can lead to progress.
But some analysts have said that re-engaging with Myanmar's new nominally civilian government, led by former junta chief turned president, Min Aung Hlaing, could weaken Asean's leverage.
Asean special envoy Maria Theresa Lazaro, the foreign minister of the Philippines, met "to discuss the way forward on an inclusive national political dialogue" with representatives of some of Myanmar rebel groups and the military-formed National Solidarity and Peacemaking Negotiation Committee, according to a Philippine foreign ministry statement.
"All sides expressed openness to the dialogue process and emphasised the importance of constructive dialogue," it said.
Several ethnic armed groups contacted by Reuters declined to comment.
The National Unity Government, a parallel administration in exile formed by remnants of the party of Aung San Suu Kyi, whose elected government was toppled in the coup, said it was not invited and expressed concern over the talks.
"We have major questions about whether this meeting is intended to implement the Asean Five-Point Consensus, or if it is based on the military junta's 100-day project and their own peace plan," the NUG's foreign minister Zin Mar Aung told Reuters.
After it took office in April, Myanmar's military-backed government announced it was seeking peace talks with opposition armed groups within 100 days.
The 2021 coup led to nationwide protests against military rule that morphed into a civil war fought on multiple fronts between the armed forces and various rebel and militia groups.
An estimated 100,000 people have been killed 3.6 million displaced.
Min Aung Hlaing was elected president in April by a parliament dominated by a pro-military party following a one-sided election earlier in the year that human rights groups and Western governments called a sham.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Helmetless riders during Johor campaign issued RTD investigation notices
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Road Transport Department (RTD) has issued notices to riders caught riding motorcycles without helmets during the recent Johor election campaign.
Johor RTD director Zulkarnain Yasin said no one would be spared from enforcement action regardless of political affiliation.
The notices require the motorcyclists to appear at any RTD office within 30 days from the date of issuance to assist investigations.
However, Zulkarnain declined to reveal the number of notices issued.
The RTD Notice 114 requires the vehicle owner or rider to attend an interview or investigation session regarding an alleged traffic offence. It is not an immediate summons or compound notice.
Zulkarnain said the action followed a viral video showing a group of election campaign workers riding motorcycles without wearing helmets during the campaign period.
"If the investigation confirms the offence, they will be issued summonses for failing to wear helmets," he said in a statement today.
He said the offence carries a compound of up to RM300, while those convicted in court could face a maximum fine of RM2,000, imprisonment of up to six months, or both.
Zulkarnain said road laws must be enforced fairly and equally, regardless of background or political affiliation.
"Any party found committing offences will face appropriate action under the law without special treatment," he said.
Earlier, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said he had directed the RTD to issue summonses to anyone found riding motorcycles without helmets during the
Johor election campaign, stressing that road laws must be enforced equally without exception.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 14 men claim trial over alleged cross-dressing, indecent dressing offences in Temerloh
Article:
TEMERLOH: Fourteen men pleaded not guilty at the Syariah Lower Court here today to charges of behaving like women at a hall in Mentakab last year.
Three of the accused also faced additional charges of dressing indecently in public.
All the accused claimed trial after the charges were read before Syariah Judge Norhisham Ahmad.
They were charged with allegedly committing the offences at a hall in Mentakab between 11pm and 11.30pm on Jan 12 last year.
The men were charged under Section 33 of the Pahang Syariah Criminal Offences Enactment 2013 for dressing or acting like women.
Three of them also faced charges under Section 53 of the same enactment for dressing indecently in public.
The offence under Section 33 carries a fine of up to RM1,000, imprisonment of up to one year, or both. The offence under Section 53 carries a fine of up to RM2,000, imprisonment of up to one year, or both.
Lawyers Muhammad Syarafi Mohd Salleh and Mohamed Noor Helmi Abdul Hamid represented the accused, while the prosecution was conducted by Busu Firdaus Abu Bakar.
The court granted bail of between RM500 and RM800 for each accused, with one surety each, and fixed Sept 22 for mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SC to recover RM5.83mil after former WCT deputy MD, director appeal dismissed
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commission (SC) will proceed to recover the judgment sum of RM5.83 million ordered by the High Court to former WCT Bhd deputy managing director Goh Chin Liong and Ara Holdings Sdn Bhd after they lost their appeal to overturn a RM5.83 million civil judgment for insider trading.
Earlier today, a three-member Court of Appeal bench comprising judges Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan, Datuk Leonard David Shim and Datuk Aliza Sulaiman unanimously dismissed the duo's appeals, affirming the High Court's ruling in favour of the SC.
The court ordered both appellants to pay RM100,000 in costs.
The court maintained the High Court's order requiring each appellant to pay RM2,542,184.70 in disgorgement of losses avoided, a RM300,000 civil penalty and RM75,000 in costs to the SC.
"Previously on May 26, 2026, the SC had succeeded in its appeal to the High Court to reinstate garnishee orders against Goh and Leong, allowing the SC to enforce recovery of the RM5.83 million judgment sum arising from the insider trading breaches by both defendants.
"Following the disposal of the appeal today, the SC will proceed with next steps to recover the judgment sum from the defendants.
"The SC views insider trading as a serious breach as it undermines the integrity of the capital market and seriously erodes investor confidence," the regulator said in a statement.
The SC first filed a civil claim against Goh and Leong in 2015 for insider trading breaches under sections 188(2) and 188(3) of the Capital Markets and Services Act 2007 (CMSA).
Evidence by the SC showed that Goh had communicated material non-public information to Leong concerning the cancellation of a contract for the proposed construction of a racecourse in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
The contract was earlier awarded to a joint venture company set up by WCT and Arabtec Construction LLC.
Leong subsequently disposed of 1.64 million WCT shares in Ara Holdings' trading account between Jan 2 and Jan 5, 2009. The then High Court judge Datuk Ahmad Zaidi Ibrahim, ruled in favour of the SC after a full trial.
"The SC will continue to pursue enforcement actions to uphold market integrity and public confidence in Malaysia's capital market."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China not seeking 'sphere of influence' with Pacific island nations, FM says
Article:
BEIJING: China "has no geopolitical intentions and does not seek a so-called 'sphere of influence'" in its dealings with Pacific island nations, Foreign Minister Wang Yi told his counterpart from the Solomon Islands in Beijing on Tuesday.
China's cooperation with the Solomon Islands "comes with no political strings attached" and is not forcibly imposed, Wang told Rick Houenipwela, an official summary released by China's foreign ministry showed.
Pacific island nations are independent and sovereign nations, "not someone's 'backyard'", Wang said, adding that China's ties with these nations "should not be subject to interference from any third party."
The meeting came a week after China's military test-fired a missile with a dummy warhead from a nuclear-powered submarine into the South Pacific, which drew criticism from regional countries, including the Solomon Islands.
The test fire coincided with the signing of a mutual defence pact between Fiji and Australia.
Solomon Islands Prime Minister Matthew Wale last week said China is "a good friend of Solomon Islands but this is not something a friend does" when asked about the missile test, while vowing stronger ties with Australia.
China is willing to enhance cooperation with the Solomon Islands including in green energy, healthcare and climate change, Wang said on Tuesday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Social Work Profession Bill tabled for second reading to regulate sector
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's social work sector is to be brought under a formal regulatory framework for the first time, with the tabling of the Social Work Profession Bill for its second reading in the Dewan Rakyat.
Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the legislation was necessary because Malaysia currently had no clear definition of social work services in any existing Act.
This left the sector without a formal mechanism to identify who was truly qualified to assist the public.
"The government and the people have no mechanism to identify who is truly qualified to provide assistance to the public beyond civil servants," she said, warning that the absence of such a framework had enabled individuals to exploit social work as a cover for harmful activities.
She cited the 2016 case of British national Richard Huckle, who entered Malaysia ostensibly to carry out social work activities and was subsequently convicted of raping and sexually assaulting 23 children in the country.
She said the Bill would establish the Malaysian Social Work Profession Council as a regulatory body to issue certificates of practice to social workers, maintain a publicly accessible register of qualified practitioners, develop professional competency standards, regulate ethics and discipline through a complaints and investigation mechanism, and promote the profession nationwide.
Nancy said Malaysia faced a severe shortage of social workers, with the current ratio standing at one social worker to every 8,576 residents — far worse than the United States at 1:490, Australia at 1:1,040, the United Kingdom at 1:3,025, and Singapore at 1:3,448.
She said the number of cases handled by the Department of Social Welfare had more than doubled over five years, rising from 663,628 cases in 2020 to 1,013,466 cases in 2025, reflecting increasingly complex social challenges facing the population.
Nancy said the ministry had been working on the legislative framework for more than 10 years, involving multiple rounds of workshops, consultation sessions, and engagement with stakeholders including government ministries and agencies, non-governmental organisations, state governments, and other parties.
She said most social work graduates were currently forced to switch to other fields such as administration and marketing due to limited career opportunities in social work, and that the Bill was intended to elevate the profession into a reputable and recognised career with a clear pathway for practitioners in both the public and private sectors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ICC prosecutor Khan loses appeal to lift suspension by British lawyers' regulator
Article:
THE HAGUE: International Criminal Court chief prosecutor Karim Khan, who has been accused of sexual misconduct, has lost an appeal to have his interim suspension lifted by the Bar Standards Board, which regulates British court lawyers, the body said on Tuesday.
Khan, a 56-year-old British lawyer, denies the allegations against him.
Khan had already been suspended from his position on June 8 by the ICC's governing body, prolonging a crisis at the war crimes court, which is also under U.S. sanctions over investigations into the United States and Israel.
The Bar Standards Board says Khan is suspended from practice as a barrister until the board or a disciplinary tribunal has ruled on the claims against him.
His supporters have suggested that he has become a political target for seeking arrest warrants for Israeli officials over Israel's conduct in its war in Gaza.
The ICC, the world's first permanent war crimes court, opened in 2002 to prosecute individuals for war crimes, crimes against humanity and genocide committed by nationals of member states or on the territory of its members.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MRL committed to implement ECRL project according to schedule
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL) is committed to completing the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) project according to the set schedule by prioritising safety, quality and public interest.
MRL in a statement today took note of media reports regarding public concerns following reports from residents of Taman Mutiara Gombak 2 regarding the safety of the construction of the Eastern Rail Link (ECRL) near their residential areas.
"MRL views every complaint and concern raised by the public seriously. Safety is always a priority in the implementation of the ECRL project," the statement said.
In this regard, MRL informed that the matter is currently going through the legal process in court and the company will not comment further to respect the judicial process.
"The construction work of the ECRL continues in accordance with the approvals, technical specifications, regulatory requirements and safety standards set by the relevant authorities," the statement said.
MRL assured that it will provide full cooperation to the court and all relevant authorities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China expels ex-Xinjiang official from Communist Party over graft
Article:
BEIJING:  China expelled a former top Xinjiang official from the ruling Communist Party and removed him from all his posts over corruption allegations, including nepotism, bribery, and paying for sex, state media said Tuesday.
Ma Xingrui, 66, was a member of the Communist Party's Politburo, a top decision-making body headed by President Xi Jinping that effectively runs the country.
The Politburo approved a report by the party's anti-corruption watchdog that set out a long list of allegations against Ma, who has been under investigation since April, state news agency Xinhua said.
The investigation found that Ma had "helped family members purchase homes at below-market prices, engaged in power-for-sex and money-for-sex transactions, and allowed family members to exploit the influence of his position to seek huge benefits", Xinhua reported.
He also "sought benefits for others in business operations, project contracting and job promotion, and illegally accepted huge amounts of money and valuables", it said, adding that his case was "particularly serious."
Ma was in charge of Xinjiang between 2021 and 2025.
The northeastern region faced a series of deadly attacks on civilians until the mid-2010s that Beijing blamed on armed separatist and Islamist groups.
Over the last decade, the region has been subject to a strict crackdown by Chinese authorities in the name of counter-terrorism, targeting primarily the Uyghur Muslim minority accused of involvement in the attacks.
Human rights organisations have condemned the crackdown.
An explosive 2022 report by former UN high commissioner for human rights Michelle Bachelet cited possible "crimes against humanity" in Xinjiang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wrong place, wrong time: Turkiye coup spells life for young cadet
Article:
ON July 15, 2016, officer cadet Alper Kalin was about to leave his base in Ankara for a friend's wedding when he was told to stay behind for an anti-terrorism exercise.
A few hours later, the efforts of a rogue military faction to overthrow the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan would fail, and Kalin would find himself facing life behind bars.
Ten years on, inside a flat in Ankara, his parents look through an album of photos of their last holiday together with their son, who is now 34.
For Kalin, a trainee air force pilot who had been selected for F-16 training at Akinci base near Ankara, the past decade has been spent inside a cell measuring 12 square metres in a prison 1,000 kilometres (600 miles) away.
Officials say he was deeply involved in the plan to oust Erdogan hatched at Akinci, where the plotters gave orders to bomb parliament and the presidential palace that night.
"There were 60 F-16 pilot candidates and they divided them into four groups. Because they didn't have the capacity to train all 60 at once, they staggered them," his brother Ilker Kalin, 35, told AFP.
By a twist of ill fate, his brother was in the first group of 14 sent to Akinci after basic training – the only ones of the 60 to be snarled up in the chaos of the coup.
Telling the cadets they were to take part in an anti-terror exercise, their superiors collected all their phones, leaving them completely clueless about events that would unfold overnight, claiming some 250 lives and leaving another 2,000 wounded.
Cut off from outside communication, they were the last to hear about the attempted coup – with Kalin's parents desperately trying to reach him as they anxiously watched the drama playing out on TV.
He finally came home around 7 am, his face "ashen, terrified", recalls his mother Kerban Kalin, 55.
Eleven days after the coup, Kalin was called back to Akinci to testify.
His father, Ali – who had only retired from a career in the military – was not worried, thinking his son would only stay back on base for one night.
At this point in the story, his wife breaks down in tears: her son was handcuffed on arrival and has never returned home.
After a four-year trial, Alper Kalin and 12 others on his training programme were sentenced on Nov 26, 2020, to life for attempting to "overthrow the constitutional order."
Statements by their commanding officers that the youngsters knew nothing did not help.
"The air force was the most actively involved. That was very unfortunate for us because Akinci base was depicted as the mastermind of the coup," said Ilker Kalin.
"They punished everybody who was at the base at that moment, no matter their rank nor what knowledge they had."
Nearly 500 people were involved in the Akinci trial, of which 484 were handed life sentences, among them 114 trainee officers.
"Hundreds of cadets went into the streets on orders of their commanders. They had absolutely no idea why," Paris-based political scientist Ahmet Insel told AFP.
At the end of 2023, Turkiye's highest appeals court upheld a sentence of aggravated life imprisonment: that is, solitary confinement with two visits a month from parents and siblings.
Each convict was sent as far from home as possible. Kalin ended up in the southeastern Kurdish-majority city of Diyarbakir, some 1,000 kilometres away from Ankara by road.
"He's not allowed to have more than five T-shirts, or to wear blue or green" – the colours of the police and the army, his brother explained.
"But he does up to 1,000 push-ups a day and reads a lot."
There are many political prisoners at that jail, which has one of Turkiye's best-stocked libraries.
With Turkiye poised to mark 10 years since the coup, the family has no expectations.
"We want this government to change. For it to correct the injustices that have torn us apart. They decided my child's fate, solving this problem is their responsibility," said his mother, a housewife turned fierce defender of civil rights.
"We're not just talking about my child here. There are hundreds and thousands of people."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Queen, Singapore First Lady tour Bangi autism centre
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, today accompanied Singapore First Lady Jane Ittogi Shanmugaratnam on a visit to the Bangi Autism Service Centre (PPA).
The visit was held in conjunction with Singapore President Tharman Shanmugaratnam's state visit to Malaysia, which began yesterday, according to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's Facebook page.
Jane Ittogi was received by Her Majesty and Tunku Tun Aminah Sultan Ibrahim upon arrival.
[[nid:1488637]]
Also present were the wife of the Prime Minister, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail; Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri; Deputy Minister Lim Hui Ying; ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Maziah Che Yusoff; and Social Welfare Department director-general Datuk Che Murad Sayang Ramjan.
[[nid:1488636]]
Raja Zarith Sofiah and Jane Ittogi toured key facilities at the centre, including the Activities of Daily Living (ADL) Room, Occupational Therapy Room and gymnasium, where they observed its autism support programmes and daily living skills training.
Nancy said 93,199 people with autism had been registered with the Social Welfare Department as of last June, with the number expected to rise as awareness and access to screening services improve.
The visit, which ended at 11.30am, further strengthened Malaysia-Singapore ties while providing an opportunity to exchange best practices in social service development.
[[nid:1488635]]
The Bangi Autism Service Centre is operated through a partnership between the public sector and non-governmental organisations, including the National Autism Society of Malaysia (Nasom) and the Damansara Damai Community-Based Rehabilitation Centre (PDK).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New US Ebola patient arrives in Germany for treatment
Article:
BERLIN: An American Ebola patient has arrived in Germany for treatment, the health ministry in Berlin said on Monday, weeks after another US citizen infected with Ebola was treated in Berlin.
The patient landed in Frankfurt overnight and was transferred to the city's university hospital, the ministry said.
The head of the World Health Organisation said the man was a "humanitarian worker" who had been in Bunia, the capital of the northeastern Ituri province of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC).
Ituri is the epicentre of the outbreak.
The WHO had provided him with "clinical care and close monitoring", WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on X.
"The patient has been safely transferred to Germany for continued follow-up care," he added.
The DRC declared its 17th Ebola outbreak in mid-May, caused by the Bundibugyo strain of the virus for which there is no vaccine or cure.
There have been over 1,900 confirmed cases in the current outbreak and more than 700 confirmed deaths, according to the WHO.
The ministry said the US patient represented "no danger for the general population or for other patients" in the Frankfurt hospital.
"The risk of someone infected with Ebola entering Germany is very low," the ministry said.
It added that US authorities had requested help from Germany because of the country's expertise in treating Ebola cases and the shorter flight time from the DRC to Germany.
Another US patient with the virus was quarantined at the end of May along with his family at Berlin's Charite Hospital.
He made a recovery after two weeks of treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 153 foreigners detained in Johor crackdown on illegal businesses
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A total of 153 foreigners were detained after the Johor Immigration Department launched a statewide crackdown on illegal business activities and immigration offences.
Johor Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the foreigners had misused Malaysia's immigration facilities to profit from unauthorised business activities.
The operation, conducted on Monday, followed surveillance and intelligence gathering on premises suspected of being operated by foreigners in violation of immigration laws.
Enforcement officers inspected businesses suspected of employing foreigners illegally or being run by migrants without approval.
[[nid:1488628]]
"Among the offences detected were misuse of the Temporary Employment Visit Pass (PLKS), overstaying and failure to produce valid travel documents," he said in a statement today.
Those detained comprised 78 Indonesians, including 21 women; 31 Bangladeshi men; 31 Myanmar nationals, including 10 women; 10 Pakistani men; two Indian men; and one Nepalese man, aged between 19 and 51.
All detainees are being investigated under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Immigration Regulations 1963 and have since been placed at the Setia Tropika Immigration Depot.
The operation also saw 29 notices issued to witnesses to assist investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 57 gored or bruised during Spain's San Fermin bull runs
Article:
PAMPLONA: Ten men suffered injuries Tuesday during the eighth and final bull run of Spain's famed San Fermin festival, bringing to 57 the total number of daredevils injured during this year's fiesta.
An 18-year-old man suffered a gore wound to the thigh and a 46-year-old man was gored in the chest, while the remaining eight were taken to hospital with bruises of varying severity, the Navarre regional government said in a statement.
The bulls completed the 848.6-metre course from a holding pen to the city bull ring in two minutes and 25 seconds.
Each morning for eight days, hundreds of daredevils, many wearing traditional white shirts with red scarves tied around their necks, tested their bravery by running ahead of a pack of bulls through the narrow, winding streets of the medieval city.
The vast majority are men.
The bulls face almost certain death in afternoon bullfights featuring Spain's top matadors.
The San Fermin festival, which was made famous by Ernest Hemingway's 1926 novel "The Sun Also Rises", draws people from around the world.
Four men who took part in this year's bull runs were gored, including a 30-year-old Spanish man who was pierced by a horn in the face.
Five of the 57 injured are foreign nationals: two Britons, an Australian, an American and a German who was gored in his left arm.
Although the runs are over, the festival's closing ceremony will take place at midnight (2200 GMT).
Sixteen people have been killed in the bull runs since records started in 1911.
The last death was in 2009 when a bull gored a 27-year-old Spaniard in the neck, heart and lungs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-WCT deputy MD, company director lose appeal in RM5.83mil insider trading case
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Former WCT Bhd deputy managing director Goh Chin Liong and Ara Holdings Sdn Bhd director Leong Ah Chai lost their appeal to overturn a RM5.83 million civil judgment for insider trading.
A three-member Court of Appeal bench comprising Justices Datuk Mohamed Zaini Mazlan, Datuk Leonard David Shim and Datuk Aliza Sulaiman unanimously dismissed the duo's appeals after finding no appealable error warranting appellate intervention, thereby affirming the High Court's ruling in favour of the Securities Commission Malaysia (SC).
It also ordered both appellants to pay RM100,000 in costs.
The court maintained the High Court's order requiring each appellant to pay RM2,542,184.70 in disgorgement of losses avoided, a RM300,000 civil penalty and RM75,000 in costs to the SC.
The SC filed the civil suit in 2015 under Sections 188(2) and 188(3) of the Capital Markets and Services Act 2007 over alleged insider trading.
According to evidence presented during the trial, Goh had communicated material non-public information to Leong regarding the cancellation of a contract for the proposed construction of a racecourse in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
The project had earlier been awarded to a joint venture between WCT and Arabtec Construction LLC.
Following the disclosure, Leong disposed of 1.64 million WCT shares through Ara Holdings' trading account between Jan 2 and Jan 5, 2009.
In a statement, the SC said it had also succeeded on May 26 in reinstating garnishee orders against Goh and Leong, enabling it to enforce recovery of the RM5.83 million judgment sum.
Following today's decision, the regulator said it would proceed with the next steps to recover the judgment sum from both defendants.
The SC described insider trading as a serious breach that undermines the integrity of the capital market and erodes investor confidence.
It said the Court of Appeal's decision sent a clear message that such misconduct would not be tolerated and reaffirmed its commitment to pursuing enforcement action to uphold market integrity and public confidence in Malaysia's capital market.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bahrain says intercepted multiple Iranian attacks
Article:
MANAMA: Bahrain on Tuesday said it intercepted several Iranian attacks on its territory, as fighting between Washington and Teheran escalated over the vital Strait of Hormuz.
"Air defence systems... intercepted and destroyed several treacherous aerial attacks launched by Iran this Tuesday morning," the Bahraini military said in a statement, also accusing Teheran of targeting civilians.
Iran said it had targeted the Gulf nation in retaliation for US attacks on Iranian territory, with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps earlier saying they had carried out missile and drone strikes on Bahrain.
Explosions were heard in the Bahraini capital, according to an AFP journalist, after warning sirens sounded for the third time since dawn.
Bahrain's defence ministry had warned on X that "sirens have been set off, we urge citizens and residents to remain calm and head to the nearest safe place."
It came hours after Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they had carried out missile and drone strikes on the Gulf kingdom.
On Monday, Bahrain's military accused Iran of targeting civilians with its latest attacks on the kingdom, after Teheran said it had struck US military facilities and infrastructure there.
"Iran continues its systematic hostile approach through its heinous attacks with missiles and drones that target civilians in the Kingdom of Bahrain," the general command of Bahrain's military said in a statement.
It also said that air defences "intercepted and destroyed a number of Iranian aerial attacks".
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia SMEs turn to digital platforms to tap China's travel recovery
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and tourism players are being encouraged to tap into China's recovering outbound travel market through digital platforms, as changing consumer behaviour creates new opportunities for Malaysian brands.
Xiaohongshu Business, in collaboration with the Malaysia-China Chamber of Commerce (MCCC), recently hosted a series of Xiaohongshu Malaysia Chinese Business Growth Summit sessions in Kuala Lumpur, Negeri Sembilan and Melaka, attracting more than 300 entrepreneurs, tourism operators and SME representatives.
The organisers said the continued recovery of China's outbound tourism market presents significant growth opportunities for Malaysia's tourism industry and SMEs, particularly those able to adapt to digital-first consumer behaviour.
In a statement, MCCC said it will continue facilitating cooperation between Malaysian and Chinese businesses across the digital economy, tourism, trade and commerce, while helping local enterprises better understand evolving Chinese consumer demand.
The three sessions formed a broader roadmap, moving from market insights in Kuala Lumpur to cultural tourism development in Negeri Sembilan and targeted industry collaboration in Melaka.
During one of the sessions in Kuala Lumpur, Xiaohongshu Business regional head of commercial cross-border Asia and Middle East Dodo Kwong shared insights on how the platform's content-driven ecosystem can help Malaysian brands reach targeted consumers in Malaysia and ASEAN markets.
In Negeri Sembilan, the discussion focused on opportunities within the tourism sector, with participants exploring ways Malaysian tourism operators could better engage Chinese travellers through digital platforms.
Negeri Sembilan Tourism, Arts and Culture Committee chairman Nicole Tan Lee Koon highlighted the potential for greater collaboration between local tourism stakeholders and digital platforms.
Xiaohongshu business representatives shared that Chinese travellers are increasingly using content platforms to research destinations before making travel decisions, with growing interest in unique local experiences, accommodation, food and cultural attractions.
The Melaka session continued the tourism focus, bringing together hotel operators, tourism attractions and restaurant owners to discuss strategies for attracting Chinese visitors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Elderly man charged with trafficking 39.9 litres of fentanyl
Article:
KANGAR: An elderly man was charged in the Magistrate's Court here today with trafficking 39.9 litres of fentanyl earlier this month.
Him Mat, 65, nodded after the charge was read out before Magistrate Ana Rozana Mohd Nor. No plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
According to the charge sheet, he was accused of trafficking the drug with another individual, who remains at large, at a house in Kampung Guar Musang, Bukit Keteri, Padang Besar, at 5.50pm on July 1.
The charge was framed under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and punishable under Section 39B(2) of the same Act, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment, and upon conviction, a minimum of 12 strokes of the cane if the death sentence is not imposed.
The accused was denied bail, and the court fixed Oct 8 for mention of the case pending submission of the chemist's report.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Alia Suzila Chek Pi appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.
—Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man shocked to discover accident victim was his own father
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A man was shocked to discover that the victim killed in an accident at the Bandar Seri Cendana traffic light intersection here today was his father.
Wan Muhammad Shah Danial Wan Mohd Shamsury, 25, was travelling along the road at about 8.40am when he witnessed the accident.
In the tragedy, his father, Wan Mohd Shamsury Wan Mohd Samsudin, 48, who is believed to have just left his home in Bandar Seri Astana for his workplace in Butterworth, Penang, was involved in the crash.
Wan Muhammad Shah Danial said he never expected his father to be involved in the accident and die at the scene.
"Before this, I only saw news reports of accidents involving high-powered motorcycle riders happening to other people, but I never expected the same thing to happen to my own father.
"All this while, I watched news about motorcycle accidents involving others, but today it happened to my own father. I was extremely shocked," he said when met at the Forensic Department of Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital here today.
Meanwhile, the victim's friend, Muhammad Azri Amir, 33, said he was shocked as he had spoken to the victim via WhatsApp the night before the incident.
He said he could not believe what had happened, describing the victim as a well-liked person who rarely spoke but was always smiling.
"The memories of riding together with him will stay with me. Whenever we rode motorcycles together, he would bring his wife along, and so would I, which brought us closer. This morning, when I found out about the incident, I was extremely shocked," he said, according to Berita Harian.
Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said the accident was believed to have occurred when a car travelling from Taman Sejati turned right into Bandar Seri Cendana and entered the motorcycle lane, where the victim was travelling straight from Bandar Perdana towards Taman Sejati.
He said the victim's motorcycle collided with the left side of the car.
"The victim fell onto the road and was confirmed dead at the scene. His body was sent to Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital, while the car driver was taken to the same hospital for further treatment," he said.
Hanyan said preliminary investigations found that the car driver had run the traffic light and entered the junction, causing the victim to collide with the left side of the vehicle.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: How flammable decor, blocked exits turned Bangkok pub into death trap
Article:
BANGKOK: At first, only thick smoke billowed out of the front entrance of the bar in northern Bangkok. It was around midnight.
Within seconds, an intense horizontal plume of fire gushed out of the doorway leading to the street, according to a video posted on social media and verified by Reuters.
Screams and shrieks filled the air.
Outside for a cigarette, Usa Tadsree at first noticed the lights flicker inside and then spotted the smoke.
"I wanted to go back in to get my friends but there was intense heat coming out, so I had to run out," said Usa, 41.
At least 30 people have died as a result of the fire and 75 are injured, 24 of them critically. The toll has once again put a spotlight on fire safety regulations and lax enforcement in Thailand's hospitality industry.
The fire erupted at 11.57pm (1657 GMT) on Sunday, probably sparked by an electrical short circuit in a ceiling air conditioner, authorities said.
Although firefighters arrived within minutes, a combination of factors proved deadly for many of the people trapped inside the Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub.
Highly combustible materials used to decorate the stage and likely for better acoustics instantly ignited to produce extreme heat, smoke and deadly toxins, choking many of those trapped inside, according to two experts who have studied the blaze.
Thai police are investigating if emergency exits were obstructed and prevented victims from escaping the burning pub, which underwent a safety inspection in April.
The pub, located at a busy intersection next to train stations and two shopping malls, is one of a cluster of similar bars often crowded on weekend nights, serving food, drinksas well as offering live music and televised sports.
The pub did not answer calls or immediately respond to a request for comment on Tuesday and its owner is among those being treated in hospital.
"The severity of this fire was driven by a massive fuel load," said Busakorn Saensookh, chair of the Fire Protection Engineering Committee at the Engineering Institute of Thailand, who inspected the site following Sunday's deadly inferno.
In particular, she pointed to highly flammable acoustic material and decorations, including artificial trees and flowers that had been installed across the ceiling to create a green canopy, especially around the stage and bar areas.
GRASS-LIKE MATERIAL, BLACK FOAM USED IN INTERIORS
Video of the venue before the fire that has been verified by Reuters shows a green grass-like material covering the ceiling above the stage, as well as what appears to be black acoustic foam panels over areas packed with seating for patrons.
"The fire involved plastic materials, and these were relatively dense plastics. Once ignited, they produced a jet of fire as enormous amounts of heat accumulated," said Busakorn.
"That heat was transferred downward, causing materials below to burn. Upholstered chairs were completely consumed by the fire."
The use of foam in the Bangkok pub's ceiling mirrors how similar material may have contributed to a devastating fire in the Swiss ski resort of Crans-Montana on New Year's Eve that killed at least 40 people and injured 119 others.
Only about five minutes after the blaze erupted, firefighter Chakrit Khongkom arrived at the scene to find the pub entirely engulfed in smoke and tried to hose down the flames coming out of the front entrance.
"Most of the survivors were choking on the smoke," Chakrit said.
A search and rescue squad that Chakrit said was sent into the pub found many victims lying around the toilets at the back of the venue, where authorities said multiple emergency exits were obstructed by tables and beer crates.
In a video shared by Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul's office during an inspection of the scene, the premier was told a door that was once an exit was bolted.
Body-camera footage from an emergency worker seen by Reuters also showed several victims lying prone on the floor near the pub's toilets.
The venue's decorative items, made from non-fire-retardant foam, produced black smoke containing carbon monoxide and cyanide that choked people trapped inside, said Amorn Pimanmas, president of the Thailand Structural Engineers Association, who has also visited the site.
"People died from inhaling toxic smoke before being burned by the fire," he said. "Several bodies showed no signs of burning."
INCOMPLETE FIRE PROTECTION SYSTEMS
The venue, like others in Bangkok, had been registered as a restaurant, although its operations resembled an entertainment establishment that has different regulatory requirements, according to Amorn and Busakorn.
"When venues are not registered as entertainment establishments, their fire protection systems are incomplete, such as lacking smoke ventilation systems," Amorn said.
"This leads to smoke and heat accumulation, creating dangerous risks."
The use of flammable materials and decorative items, crowded conditions and unusable emergency exits in Thai establishments have been previously flagged, including after a 2022 nightclub fire in Chonburi that killed 13 people and a 2009 blaze at another Bangkok nightclub that killed at least 65.
Since Sunday's fire, Bangkok city authorities have said they plan to review multiple regulations, including the use of decorative materials and the definition of entertainment establishments.
"The risks are much greater today," Busakorn said, "But we are still using the same laws written 30 to 40 years ago that no longer reflect current realities." --REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman dies, husband injured after motorcycle hits wild boar in Bera
Article:
BERA: A 48-year-old pillion rider died and her husband was injured after their motorcycle collided with a wild boar at Km35 of Jalan Temerloh–Bahau yesterday.
Nor Harizan Muhamad Habri, who was travelling with her 50-year-old husband on a Yamaha motorcycle, succumbed to severe head injuries while receiving treatment at Sultan Haji Ahmad Shah Hospital (Hoshas) in Temerloh about 4.25am today.
Bera police chief Superintendent Zulkiflee Nazir said the couple were travelling from Nilai, Negri Sembilan, to Charuk Puting, Temerloh, when the accident occurred about 12.30pm yesterday.
"Initial investigations revealed that the motorcycle crashed into a wild boar that suddenly crossed the single-carriageway. The impact threw the rider and his wife onto the road.
"They were taken to Bera Hospital before Nor Harizan was transferred to Hoshas, where she succumbed to her injuries several hours later," he said in a statement today.
Harizan's husband remains hospitalised at Bera Hospital with rib injuries.
Zulkiflee said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
The wild boar is believed to have fled into a nearby oil palm plantation after the collision.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Melaka clears path to appoint seven unelected assemblymen
Article:
AYER KEROH: The Melaka State Legislative Assembly today passed an amendment to the State Constitution to allow the appointment of up to seven assemblymen.
The motion, tabled by Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh, was supported by 23 assemblymen, including three from the opposition.
Five Pakatan Harapan (PH) representatives — four from DAP and one from Amanah — voted against the amendment.
The amendment allows the Melaka government to appoint up to seven assemblymen from various backgrounds, as provided for under the Bill.
Under the amended Article 11 of the Melaka State Constitution, the existing 28 elected assemblymen will remain unchanged, in line with the Federal Constitution.
When tabling the Bill, Ab Rauf said the amendment was aimed at strengthening representation in the state assembly by enabling individuals with expertise in areas such as law, economics, education, investment, technology and state development to contribute directly to policymaking and legislative debates.
He said it would also improve representation for groups that are often underrepresented, including women, youths, Orang Asli, minority communities, professionals and industry representatives who may not have been elected but could contribute to the state's development.
The appointment of assemblymen with specialised expertise would also enhance the quality of debates and strengthen checks and balances by providing more technical, professional and objective input on Bills, government policies and state administration, he said.
Ab Rauf said the move would also support the state's development agenda by enabling the government to tap the expertise of experienced individuals in strategic sectors to help implement development policies, attract investment and enhance Melaka's competitiveness.
He added that the move was in line with practices adopted in several other states, whose constitutions provide for the appointment of assemblymen or additional members to strengthen legislative functions and ensure broader representation.
Ab Rauf said each appointed assemblyman must first be approved through a motion supported by at least seven assemblymen and passed by a simple majority before being appointed by the Yang di-Pertua Negeri.
He said only individuals residing in Melaka would be eligible for appointment.
"The appointed assemblyman's seat will be vacated if they submit a resignation letter, are removed through a motion supported by at least seven assemblymen and passed by a simple majority, if the state assembly is dissolved, or if their appointment is terminated under Article 11B of the Melaka State Constitution, whichever comes first.
"Appointed assemblymen have the right to speak, participate in debates and assembly committees, and vote, subject to the prescribed limits. They enjoy the same rights and privileges as elected assemblymen, but are not eligible to be appointed as Chief Minister or State Executive Councillor (Exco)," he said.
Meanwhile, Ab Rauf said the Yang di-Pertua Negeri, acting on the advice of the Chief Minister, may terminate the appointment of an appointed assemblyman without giving any reason if it is deemed necessary in the interests of the country or state, national security, government policy or public policy.
He added that decisions on the matter fall under the jurisdiction of the state government and are final.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor MIC credits groundwork for election clean sweep
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor MIC's clean sweep of all four contested seats in the July 11 state election was the result of consistent groundwork and engagement with the community over the past four years, said party chief K. Raven Kumar.
The newly elected Kemelah assemblyman said the party began strengthening its presence on the ground after the 2022 state election, allowing it to secure victories despite fielding three new faces in the July 11 polls.
MIC won all four seats it contested — Kemelah, Kahang, Perling and Bukit Batu — with Raven saying the party's presence was felt across the state, particularly in Johor Baru, Kluang, Segamat and Kulai, where the Indian community population is significant.
"MIC is a party under Barisan Nasional, but our main role is to look after the Indian community. That is why we focused our efforts in these areas," he told the New Straits Times today.
Raven said voters today were more rational and no longer made choices based solely on sentiments or emotions.
"Voters are smart now. They judge based on performance and what has been delivered," he said.
He said MIC was now focusing on educating the community to ensure their concerns could be channelled directly to the relevant representatives.
Raven said several issues raised by the opposition during the campaign, including water supply problems, had already been addressed after they were brought to the state government's attention.
"The issue was resolved immediately when it came to our attention," he said.
However, he acknowledged that some residents were still unaware of how to reach their representatives despite MIC having a strong division and grassroots presence.
"We are now looking at strengthening our digital presence so that we are available at their fingertips," he said.
On whether he would be reappointed to the state executive council (Exco), Raven said the decision remained the prerogative of Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi and the Johor royal institution.
"If given the opportunity, I will continue working hard to serve the people. If not, I am willing to guide whoever is appointed," he said.
"We respect the BN system in deciding the Exco line-up and we are not challenging that. What is important is that MIC can continue serving the people," he said.
He also attributed BN's strong performance in winning 48 of the 56 state seats to the state government's track record over the past four years rather than the two-week campaign period.
"The secret to this victory is that the previous Exco line-up did a good job. Voters judged based on the government's performance over four years," he said.
Raven reminded newly elected representatives to remain grounded despite the strong mandate.
"My advice to the winners is not to keep their heads in the clouds. Return to the ground because five years pass very quickly," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kia Malaysia expands dealer network with Cycle & Carriage flagship centre
Article:
CYCLE & Carriage (C&C) has opened its first Kia flagship 3S centre in Malaysia, marking its entry into the Korean marque's retail network after being appointed an authorised dealer partner by Kia Sales Malaysia.
Located in Glenmarie, Shah Alam, the facility combines vehicle sales, aftersales services and maintenance under one roof as Kia expands its customer support network in the country.
The 20,839 sq ft centre includes a 5,511 sq ft showroom capable of displaying up to five vehicles, a service centre, workshop, parts operations and a 2,067 sq ft customer lounge.
The workshop is equipped with 20 service bays and infrastructure that is ready to support electric vehicle (EV) charging requirements as demand for electrified models grows.
C&C said the outlet is designed in line with Kia's latest global retail standards, featuring digital displays, a dedicated vehicle display area, consultation spaces, an integrated service reception and customer lounge.
C&C Malaysia chief executive officer Adrian Short said the new outlet reflects the company's strategy of strengthening aftersales support alongside vehicle sales.
He said the partnership with Kia Malaysia will enable the company to provide customers with more convenient access to sales and servicing throughout the ownership cycle.
Kia Malaysia managing director Emily Lek said the company is working to strengthen its dealership and aftersales network as part of efforts to enhance the overall ownership experience.
She said C&C's automotive experience and service capabilities would support Kia's plans to expand its presence in Malaysia.
The Glenmarie outlet adds to Kia's growing retail and aftersales footprint, providing customers with a centralised facility for vehicle purchases, servicing and maintenance as the brand broadens its operations in the local market.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pekan Sehari Temerloh closure claim false, says council
Article:
TEMERLOH: The Temerloh Municipal Council has denied claims that it instructed the closure of the popular Pekan Sehari Temerloh for three weeks.
The council, in a Facebook post, said a viral social media claim about the market's closure was false and that no instruction had been issued to suspend its operations.
"The Temerloh Municipal Council wishes to clarify that a viral social media post claiming the 'pekan sehari' will be closed to visitors for three weeks is untrue. The council has never made such a statement.
"The 'pekan sehari' is operating as usual. The public is advised not to spread unverified information, as it could cause confusion and affect traders and visitors," read the post.
The public has been urged to obtain verified information only from the council's official platforms, namely its Facebook page at https://www.facebook.com/majlisperbandarantemerloh, TikTok (@mptemerloh), Instagram (@majlisperbandarantemerloh), and official website at http://www.mpt.gov.my/.
The decades-old Pekan Sehari Temerloh, located in Lurah Semantan, stretches 2.2km and is now the country's second longest morning market after Sungai Bayor morning market in Selama, Perak, was recognised last year as the longest at 2.3km.
Pekan Sehari Temerloh was inducted into the Malaysian Book of Records in 2023 for having the longest morning market and the highest number of traders.
The market, which operates every Sunday from 7am to 1.30pm, has around 600 hawkers.
The outdoor market offers a wide variety of products, including rare traditional kuih and freshwater fish caught in Sungai Pahang. It attracts thousands of visitors from across Pahang and the Klang Valley every week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KL police nab suspect linked to Chan Sow Lin gang robbery
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a man believed to be involved in a gang robbery at a condominium unit along Jalan Dua, Chan Sow Lin, in March 2024.
Cheras district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Rosdi Daud said the suspect, who is in his late 20s, was arrested by a team from the Cheras district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department (D9) Special Investigation Division at about noon on July 10.
"Acting on intelligence and information gathered, the D9 team successfully arrested the suspect by the roadside in Sungai Jelok, Kajang.
"We also seized a pair of jeans and shoes believed to have been worn by the suspect during the robbery," he said in a statement today.
Mohd Rosdi said the suspect had been remanded for seven days from July 11 until July 17 to facilitate investigations.
"The case is being investigated under Section 395 of the Penal Code for gang robbery, and further investigations are ongoing," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid to launch BN election machinery, candidate lineup for Negri polls tomorrow night
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional will launch its election machinery and announce its candidates for the Negri Sembilan state election tomorrow.
The event, to be officiated by BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, will be held at 9pm at the Tuanku Abdul Rahman Stadium car park in Paroi, Seremban, according to a media advisory issued by Umno.
Yesterday, Zahid, who is also Umno president, said BN would adopt a formula
tailored
to the political landscape of Negri Sembilan, taking into account the state's unique demographics and voter patterns.
"This is not just about candidates. The situation in Negri Sembilan is certainly different because the number of seats, population and voter demographics are different.
"So, the formula must be adjusted according to the political situation in Negri Sembilan. We will announce the candidates this week," he said after officiating the TVET@KKDW contingent heading to the WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 competition.
The Election Commission has set
Aug 1
as polling day for the 16th Negri Sembilan election, with all 36 state seats to be contested.
In the 2023 election, Pakatan Harapan won 17 seats, with DAP taking 11, PKR five and Parti Amanah Negara one. BN won 14 seats, while Perikatan Nasional took five.
BN retained its position in the 16th Johor election held two days ago.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MRL declines comment on ECRL safety claims as case goes to court
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL) will not comment further on safety concerns raised by residents of Taman Mutiara Gombak 2 over the construction of the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) near their homes, as the matter is now before the courts.
"MRL will not comment on matters that are the subject of the proceedings, in respect of the judicial process," the company said in a statement, adding that it would continue to give its full cooperation to the court and all relevant authorities.
MRL said it had taken note of media reports on the residents' concerns and stressed that safety remained its priority in implementing the ECRL project.
"MRL takes every complaint and concern raised by the public seriously.
"Safety remains a priority in the implementation of the ECRL project," it said.
The company said construction works were being carried out in accordance with the necessary approvals, technical specifications, regulatory requirements and safety standards set by the relevant authorities.
It added that it remained committed to completing the project on schedule while prioritising safety, quality and the public interest.
Previously,
the New Straits Times reported
that a family had filed a RM1.33 million lawsuit against three parties involved in the ECRL project, alleging that construction works had compromised the safety and structural integrity of their home.
The suit named China Communications Construction (ECRL) Sdn Bhd, MRL and the Selayang Municipal Council as defendants.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: In Muhyiddin’s trial, businessman says brother donated part of company’s RM13.7m to Bersatu without his knowledge
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — Businessman Shahrin Shamsuddin today told the High Court that he only discovered his elder brother, Shahradzi, had used part of their family-operated company’s RM13.772 million to donate to Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) after the donations had already been made.
Shahrin said this while testifying as the 15th prosecution witness in Bersatu president and former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial.
He said he had intended the money to be used instead to pay the operating costs and outstanding debts of several family-owned companies.
Shahrin is the managing director and owner of Bumiputera construction company Sutracom Sdn Bhd, which was awarded a RM605.2 million contract in October 2022 to build and upgrade a highway in Pulau Indah, Selangor under the government’s Jana Wibawa programme.
After receiving security deposits totalling RM14.5 million from Sutracom’s subcontractors in 10 transactions between September 2021 and November 2022, Shahrin transferred RM13.772 million from Sutracom to his brother’s company, Mamfor Sdn Bhd, through nine transactions during the same period.
Shahrin said it was only during a family meeting to discuss the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission’s (MACC) investigation into Sutracom that he learnt Shahradzi had used part of the RM13.772 million transferred to Mamfor to make donations to Bersatu.
However, he said he did not know how much had been donated.
“I’m not sure of the breakdown of the total funds that Shahradzi used to pay the family companies’ debts and operating costs and to donate to PPBM as he never informed me, and I have given the trust to him to handle the settling of my family’s companies’ old debts and operating costs,” he told the High Court.
Asked by deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Jumaat, Shahrin said his elder brother had never told him what the RM13.7 million would be used for or that any of it would be donated to Bersatu.
He also said he had never given permission for any of the RM13.7 million to be donated to Bersatu.
Shahrin said neither he nor his elder brother belonged to any political party, and that he personally had never donated to any political party, including Bersatu.
Asked by High Court judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin about his own political contributions, Shahrin said he preferred to “donate” by casting his vote after assessing political parties’ policies, direction and the credibility of their candidates.
Previously in the trial, Mamfor was revealed to have donated RM19.5 million to Bersatu through 29 cheques between August 2021 and November 2022. A bank officer testified that the transactions were unusual because the company had repeatedly issued RM500,000 cheques on the same day.
About transferring money among family’s companies and letting brother manage debts
Shahrin said the RM13.7 million was intended to settle debts owed by Sutracom, Mamfor – wholly owned by Shahradzi – and Bumiputera construction company Majulia Sdn Bhd, which is owned by his younger brother and mother.
Although the companies had different registered addresses and were headed by different family members, Shahrin said his elder brother had taken the initiative during the Covid-19 pandemic to manage the family companies’ debts by dealing with creditors.
Explaining why he had no reason to question transferring Sutracom’s RM13.7 million to Mamfor as an “advance”, Shahrin said the amount was within the range of the companies’ total debts and that he believed his elder brother was simply carrying out his responsibility.
Under questioning from defence lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, Shahrin said that it was “common” and “not unusual” for the family-owned companies to transfer money among themselves.
Although Sutracom, Mamfor and Majulia are each owned by different family members, Shahrin said he still regarded them as family companies.
For example, although he owns 100 per cent of Sutracom’s shares according to Companies Commission of Malaysia (SSM) records, he said the company belongs to the business itself, and that his shareholding simply meant he bore full responsibility for its operations.
Former Prime Minister, Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin is pictured arrives at the Kuala Lumpur High Court Complex on July 14, 2026. — Picture by Yusof Isa
Shahrin says Muhyiddin never contacted him, does not know then-PM personally
Earlier, Shahrin said his elder brother had prepared a letter dated March 5, 2021 to then-prime minister Muhyiddin expressing Sutracom’s interest in joining the pre-qualification tender for the Pulau Indah project, using Sutracom’s letterhead and signing on his behalf for convenience.
He later explained that the letter merely introduced the company’s intention to apply for the project and did not guarantee it would be awarded the contract, as the procurement process still involved tenders or quotations.
Under questioning from another defencelawyer Datuk K. Kumaraendran, Shahrin said he did not personally know Muhyiddin.
He also confirmed that Muhyiddin had no connection with, or financial interest in, Sutracom, Mamfor or Majulia.
Shahrin said Sutracom won the RM605,217,790 Pulau Indah project on its own merits, as he believed the company had the financial strength, technical capability, experience, track record and qualified personnel required.
Based on former finance minister Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz’s earlier testimony, the Pulau Indah project was among the Jana Wibawa projects that Muhyiddin had approved to be awarded through a pre-qualification tender.
When questioned by Amer Hamzah, Shahrin confirmed he had never received any phone calls from Muhyiddin or officers from the Prime Minister’s Office during the contractor evaluation process.
He also agreed that Muhyiddin had never asked about Sutracom’s application for the project or whether the company had received interim payments from the government for completed work.
Shahrin confirmed he had never contacted Muhyiddin personally, nor had Muhyiddin ever contacted him personally.
He added that Sutracom has completed 83.3 per cent of the Pulau Indah project and is ahead of schedule.
In this trial, Muhyiddin faces seven charges — four counts of allegedly abusing his position to obtain RM225.3 million in bribes for Bersatu (from Nepturis Sdn Bhd, Azman Yusoff, Bukhary Equity Sdn Bhd and Mamfor Sdn Bhd), and three counts of money laundering involving funds Bersatu allegedly received from Bukhary Equity.
The trial resumes tomorrow.
